title: delphinium consolida subsp. arvense
intro: Delphinium consolida subsp. arvense, also known as field larkspur, is a perennial plant species native to Europe and parts of Asia. It is a member of the borage family and is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and antispasmodic properties. The plant is rich in flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, providing health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health. Field larkspur can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat a range of ailments, including fever, digestive issues, and skin problems.
